codeSystems.name: PracticeSettingCodeValueSet

codeSystems.description: This is the code representing the clinical specialty of the clinician or provider who interacted with, treated, or provided a service to/for the patient. The value set used for clinical specialty has been limited by HITSP to the value set reproduced from HITSP C80 Table 2-149 Clinical Specialty Value Set Definition.

codeSystems.name: FHIRDeviceTypes
codeSystems.title: FHIR Device Types
codeSystems.description: Codes used to identify medical devices. Includes concepts from SNOMED CT (http://www.snomed.org/) where concept is-a 49062001 (Device)  and is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: DeviceNameType
codeSystems.title: DeviceNameType
codeSystems.description: The type of name the device is referred by.

codeSystems.name: FHIRDeviceStatus
codeSystems.title: FHIRDeviceStatus
codeSystems.description: The availability status of the device.

codeSystems.name: FHIRDeviceStatusReason
codeSystems.title: FHIRDeviceStatusReason
codeSystems.description: The availability status reason of the device.

codeSystems.name: DeviceType
codeSystems.title: Device Type
codeSystems.description: Codes used to identify medical devices. Includes concepts from SNOMED CT (http://www.snomed.org/) where concept is-a 49062001 (Device)  and is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: DiagnosisRole
codeSystems.title: DiagnosisRole
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to express the role of a diagnosis on the Encounter or EpisodeOfCare record.

codeSystems.name: DiagnosticReportStatus
codeSystems.title: DiagnosticReportStatus
codeSystems.description: The status of the diagnostic report.

codeSystems.name: DiagnosticServiceSectionCodes
codeSystems.title: Diagnostic Service Section Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set includes all the codes in HL7 V2 table 0074.

codeSystems.name: DietCodes
codeSystems.title: Diet Codes
codeSystems.description: Codes that can be used to indicate the type of food being ordered for a patient. This value set is provided as a suggestive example. It includes codes from [SNOMED CT](http://snomed.info/sct) where concept is-a 182922004 (Dietary regime (regime/therapy))

codeSystems.name: DocumentClassValueSet
codeSystems.title: Document Class Value Set
codeSystems.description: This is the code specifying the high-level kind of document (e.g. Prescription, Discharge Summary, Report, etc.). Note: Class code for documents comes from LOINC, and is based upon one of the following:The type of service described by the document. It is described at a very high level in Section 7.3 of the LOINC Manual. The type study performed. It was determined by identifying modalities for study reports. The section of the chart where the document is placed. It was determined from the SETs created for Claims Attachment requests.

codeSystems.name: DocumentReferenceStatus
codeSystems.title: DocumentReferenceStatus
codeSystems.description: The status of the document reference.

codeSystems.name: DocumentRelationshipType
codeSystems.title: DocumentRelationshipType
codeSystems.description: The type of relationship between documents.

codeSystems.name: DoseAndRateType
codeSystems.title: DoseAndRateType
codeSystems.description: The kind of dose or rate specified.

codeSystems.name: AdmitSource
codeSystems.title: Admit source
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate from where the patient came in.

codeSystems.name: Diet
codeSystems.title: Diet
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate dietary preferences or restrictions a patient may have.

codeSystems.name: EncounterLocationStatus
codeSystems.title: EncounterLocationStatus
codeSystems.description: The status of the location.

codeSystems.name: ParticipantType
codeSystems.title: Participant type
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate how an individual participates in an encounter.

codeSystems.name: EncounterReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: Encounter Reason Codes
codeSystems.description: This examples value set defines the set of codes that can be used to indicate reasons for an encounter.

codeSystems.name: SpecialArrangements
codeSystems.title: Special arrangements
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate the kinds of special arrangements in place for a patients visit.

codeSystems.name: SpecialCourtesy
codeSystems.title: Special courtesy
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to indicate special courtesies provided to the patient.

codeSystems.name: EncounterStatus
codeSystems.title: EncounterStatus
codeSystems.description: Current state of the encounter.

codeSystems.name: EndpointConnectionType
codeSystems.title: Endpoint Connection Type
codeSystems.description: This is an example value set defined by the FHIR project, that could be used to represent possible connection type profile values.

codeSystems.name: EndpointPayloadType
codeSystems.title: Endpoint Payload Type
codeSystems.description: This is an example value set defined by the FHIR project, that could be used to represent possible payload document types.

codeSystems.name: EndpointStatus
codeSystems.title: EndpointStatus
codeSystems.description: The status of the endpoint.

codeSystems.name: EnteralRouteCodes
codeSystems.title: Enteral Route Codes
codeSystems.description: EnteralRouteOfAdministration: Codes specifying the route of administration of enteral formula.  This value set is composed of HL7 V3 codes and is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: EnteralFormulaAdditiveTypeCode
codeSystems.title: Enteral Formula Additive Type Code
codeSystems.description: EnteralFormulaAdditiveType: Codes for the type of modular component such as protein, carbohydrate or fiber to be provided in addition to or mixed with the base formula. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: EnteralFormulaTypeCodes
codeSystems.title: Enteral Formula Type Codes
codeSystems.description: EnteralFormulaType : Codes for type of enteral formula to be administered to patient.  This value set is composed of SNOMED CT (US Extension) Concepts from SCTID 470581016 (Enteral+supplement feeds hierarchy (product)) and is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: EpisodeOfCareStatus
codeSystems.title: EpisodeOfCareStatus
codeSystems.description: The status of the episode of care.

codeSystems.name: EpisodeOfCareType
codeSystems.title: Episode of care type
codeSystems.description: This example value set defines a set of codes that can be used to express the usage type of an EpisodeOfCare record.

codeSystems.name: EventStatus
codeSystems.title: EventStatus
codeSystems.description: Codes identifying the lifecycle stage of an event.

codeSystems.name: EventTiming
codeSystems.title: EventTiming
codeSystems.description: Real world event relating to the schedule.

codeSystems.name: FinancialResourceStatusCodes
codeSystems.title: Financial Resource Status Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set includes Status codes.

codeSystems.name: FoodTypeCodes
codeSystems.title: Food Type Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set represents codes for types of foods and is provided as a suggestive example. It include codes from [SNOMED CT](http://snomed.info/sct) where concept is-a 255620007 (Foods (substance)).

codeSystems.name: GoalAchievementStatus
codeSystems.title: Goal achievement status
codeSystems.description: Describes the progression, or lack thereof, towards the goal against the target.

codeSystems.name: GoalCategory
codeSystems.title: Goal category
codeSystems.description: Example codes for grouping goals to use for filtering or presentation.

codeSystems.name: GoalPriority
codeSystems.title: Goal priority
codeSystems.description: Indicates the level of importance associated with reaching or sustaining a goal.

codeSystems.name: GoalStartEvent
codeSystems.title: Goal start event
codeSystems.description: Identifies types of events that might trigger the start of a goal.

codeSystems.name: GoalLifecycleStatus
codeSystems.title: GoalLifecycleStatus
codeSystems.description: Codes that reflect the current state of a goal and whether the goal is still being targeted.

codeSystems.name: GroupType
codeSystems.title: GroupType
codeSystems.description: Types of resources that are part of group.

codeSystems.name: Identifier Type Codes
codeSystems.title: IdentifierType
codeSystems.description: A coded type for an identifier that can be used to determine which identifier to use for a specific purpose.

codeSystems.name: IdentifierUse
codeSystems.title: IdentifierUse
codeSystems.description: Identifies the purpose for this identifier, if known .

codeSystems.name: ImagingStudyStatus
codeSystems.title: ImagingStudyStatus
codeSystems.description: The status of the ImagingStudy.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationEvaluationDoseStatusCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Evaluation Dose Status codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the validity of a dose relative to a particular recommended schedule. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationEvaluationDoseStatusReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Evaluation Dose Status Reason codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why an administered dose has been assigned a particular status. Often, this reason describes why a dose is considered invalid. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationEvaluationStatusCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Evaluation Status Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the current status of the evaluation for vaccine administration event.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationEvaluationTargetDiseaseCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Evaluation Target Disease Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the disease that the evaluation is against. This value set is provided as a suggestive example and includes the SNOMED CT concepts from the 64572001 (Disease) hierarchy.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationFunctionCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Function Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the function a practitioner or organization may play in the immunization event. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationFundingSource
codeSystems.title: Immunization Funding Source
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the source of the vaccine administered. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationOriginCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Origin Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the source of the data when the report of the immunization event is not based on information from the person, entity or organization who administered the vaccine. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationProgramEligibility
codeSystems.title: Immunization Program Eligibility
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the patient's eligibility for a vaccination program. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Reason Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why a dose of vaccine was administered. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationRecommendationDateCriterionCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Recommendation Date Criterion Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support the definition of dates relevant to recommendations for future doses of vaccines. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationRecommendationReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Recommendation Reason Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reasons why a given recommendation status is assigned. This value set is provided as a suggestive example and includes SNOMED CT concepts.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationRecommendationStatusCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Recommendation Status Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the status of the patient towards perceived immunity against a vaccine preventable disease. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationRecommendationTargetDiseaseCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Recommendation Target Disease Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the disease targeted by a vaccination recommendation. This value set is provided as a suggestive example and includes the SNOMED CT concepts from the 64572001 (Disease) hierarchy.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationRouteCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Route Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the administrative routes used during vaccination. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: CodesForImmunizationSiteOfAdministration
codeSystems.title: Codes for Immunization Site of Administration
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the body site where the vaccination occurred. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationStatusCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Status Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the current status of the administered dose of vaccine.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationStatusReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Status Reason Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why a dose of vaccine was not administered. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationSubpotentReason
codeSystems.title: Immunization Subpotent Reason
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the reason why a dose is considered to be subpotent. This value set is provided as a suggestive example.

codeSystems.name: ImmunizationTargetDiseaseCodes
codeSystems.title: Immunization Target Disease Codes
codeSystems.description: The value set to instantiate this attribute should be drawn from a terminologically robust code system that consists of or contains concepts to support describing the disease that the dose is being administered against. This value set is provided as a suggestive example and includes the SNOMED CT concepts from the 64572001 (Disease) hierarchy.

codeSystems.name: ObservationInterpretationCodes
codeSystems.title: Observation Interpretation Codes
codeSystems.description:
A categorical assessment, providing a rough qualitative interpretation of the observation value,    such as “normal”/ “abnormal”,”low” / “high”, “better” / “worse”, “susceptible” / “resistant”, “expected”/ “not expected”.    The value set is intended to be for ANY use where coded representation of an interpretation is needed.   
   
   Notes:
   
   This is being communicated in v2.x in OBX-8 (Observation Interpretation), in v3 in ObservationInterpretation (CWE) in R1 (Representative Realm) and in FHIR in    Observation.interpretation. Historically these values come from the laboratory domain, and these codes are extensively    used. The value set binding is extensible, so codes outside the value set that are needed for interpretation concepts    (i.e. particular meanings) that are not included in the value set can be used, and these new codes may also be added to    the value set and published in a future version.

codeSystems.name: ObservationMethods

codeSystems.description: Observation Method codes from [SNOMED CT](http://snomed.info/sct) where concept is-a 272394005 (Technique (qualifier value)) or is-a 129264002 (Action (qualifier value)) or is-a 386053000 (Evaluation procedure(procedure))

codeSystems.description: Situation codes describing the reason that a procedure, which might otherwise be expected, was not performed, or a procedure that was started and was not completed. Consists of SNOMED CT codes, children of procedure contraindicated (183932001), procedure discontinued (416406003), procedure not done (416237000), procedure not indicated (428119001), procedure not offered (416064006), procedure not wanted (416432009), procedure refused (183944003), and procedure stopped (394908001).

codeSystems.name: VisionEyes

codeSystems.name: ExampleVisionPrescriptionProductCodes
codeSystems.title: Example Vision Prescription Product Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set includes a smattering of Prescription Product codes.

codeSystems.name: LanguageCodesWithLanguageAndOptionallyARegionModifier
codeSystems.title: Language codes with language and optionally a region modifier
codeSystems.description: This value set includes codes from [BCP-47](http://tools.ietf.org/html/bcp47). This value set matches the ONC 2015 Edition LanguageCommunication data element value set within C-CDA to use a 2 character language code if one exists,   and a 3 character code if a 2 character code does not exist. It points back to [RFC 5646](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5646), however only the language codes are required, all other elements are optional.

codeSystems.name: USCoreClinicalResultObservationCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core Clinical Result Observation Category
codeSystems.description: Used to classify the context of clinical result observations.

codeSystems.name: USCoreConditionCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Condition Codes
codeSystems.description: This describes the problem. Diagnosis/Problem List is broadly defined as a series of brief statements that catalog a patient's medical, nursing, dental, social, preventative and psychiatric events and issues that are relevant to that patient's healthcare (e.g., signs, symptoms, and defined conditions). ICD-10 is appropriate for Diagnosis information, and ICD-9 for historical information.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDiagnosticReportCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core Diagnostic Report Category Codes
codeSystems.description: The US Core Diagnostic Report Category Value Set is a 'starter set' of categories supported for fetching and Diagnostic Reports and notes.

codeSystems.name: USCoreNonLaboratoryCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Non Laboratory Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set includes All LOINC codes defined as type "clinical," therefore excluding laboratory tests, survey instruments, and claims documents. This includes observables such as vital signs, hemodynamics, intake/output, EKG, obstetric ultrasound, and cardiac echo, and includes discrete and narrative diagnostic observations and reports.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDocumentReferenceCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core DocumentReference Category
codeSystems.description: The US Core DocumentReferences Category Value Set is a 'starter set' of categories supported for fetching and storing clinical notes.

codeSystems.name: USCoreDocumentReferenceType
codeSystems.title: US Core DocumentReference Type
codeSystems.description: The US Core DocumentReference Type Value Set includes all LOINC values whose SCALE is "Doc" in the LOINC database and the HL7 v3 Code System NullFlavor concept 'unknown'

codeSystems.name: USCoreEncounterType
codeSystems.title: US Core Encounter Type
codeSystems.description:
The type of encounter: a specific code indicating type of service provided. This value set includes codes from SNOMED CT decending from the concept 308335008 (Patient encounter procedure (procedure)) and codes from the Current Procedure and Terminology (CPT) found in the following CPT sections:
- 99211-99499 E/M - 99500-99600 home health (mainly nonphysician, such as newborn care in home) - 99605-99607 medication management - 98966-98968 non physician telephone services
(subscription to AMA Required)

codeSystems.name: USCoreGoalCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Goal Codes
codeSystems.description: Concepts from CPT and LOINC code systems that can be used to indicate the goal.

codeSystems.name: USCoreLaboratoryTestCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Laboratory Test Codes
codeSystems.description: Laboratory LOINC codes: From the introduction to August 2022 LOINC Users' guide; "The current scope of the existing laboratory portion of the LOINC  database includes all observations reported by clinical laboratories, including the specialty areas: chemistry, including therapeutic drug monitoring and toxicology; hematology; serology; blood bank; microbiology; cytology; surgical pathology; and fertility.

codeSystems.name: NarrativeStatus
codeSystems.title: US Core Narrative Status
codeSystems.description: The US Core Narrative Status Value Set limits the text status for the resource narrative.

codeSystems.name: USCoreProblemOrHealthConcern
codeSystems.title: US Core Problem or Health Concern
codeSystems.description: Code set for category codes for *US Core Condition Problems and Health Concerns Profile* consisting of the concepts "problem" and "health-concern".

codeSystems.name: USCoreProcedureCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core Procedure Codes
codeSystems.description: Concepts from CPT, SNOMED CT, HCPCS Level II Alphanumeric Codes, ICD-10-PCS,CDT and LOINC code systems that can be used to indicate the type of procedure performed.

codeSystems.name: USCoreScreeningAssessmentConditionCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core Screening Assessment Condition Category
codeSystems.description: Category codes used in the US Core Condition Screening Assessment Profile to help identify the type of USCDI Health Status/Assessment data class being reported.

codeSystems.name: USCoreServiceRequestCategoryCodes
codeSystems.title: US Core ServiceRequest Category Codes
codeSystems.description: A set of SNOMED CT and LOINC concepts to classify a requested service

codeSystems.name: USCoreSimpleObservationCategory
codeSystems.title: US Core Simple Observation Category
codeSystems.description: Used to classify the context of a survey, screening or assessment for simple observations and may be used to assist with indexing and searching for appropriate instances.

codeSystems.name: UspsTwoLetterAlphabeticCodes
codeSystems.title: USPS Two Letter Alphabetic Codes
codeSystems.description: This value set defines two letter USPS alphabetic codes.

codeSystems.name: ClinicalDischargeDisposition
codeSystems.title: Clinical Discharge Disposition
codeSystems.description: This value set defines a set of codes that can be used to where the patient left the hospital. Note that this value set explicitly removes 'oth' (Other) to allow the binding strength to be extensible and therefore allow the exchange of additional concepts without requiring mapping to 'oth.'

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSReAdmissionIndicator
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-re-admissionIndicator
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes which are used to specify that a patient is being re-admitted to a healthcare facility from which they were discharged, and indicates the circumstances around such re-admission.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSBedStatus
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-bedStatus
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify the state of a bed in an inpatient setting, and is used to determine if a patient may be assigned to it or not.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSAppointmentReasonCodes
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-appointmentReasonCodes
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that describe the kind of appointment or the reason why an appointment has been scheduled.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSAdditivePreservative
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-additivePreservative
codeSystems.description: Concepts specifying any additive introduced to the specimen before or at the time of collection.  These additives may be introduced in order to preserve, maintain or enhance the particular nature or component of the specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSSpecimenCondition
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-specimenCondition
codeSystems.description: Concepts of modes or states of being that describe the nature of a specimen.  Used in Version 2 messaging in the SPM segment.

codeSystems.name: Hl7VSRelevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.title: hl7VS-relevantClincialInformation
codeSystems.description: Value Set of codes that specify additional clinical information about the patient or specimen to report the supporting and/or suspected diagnosis and clinical findings on requests for interpreted diagnostic studies.

codeSystems.name: v2.0360.2.7
codeSystems.title: v2 table 0360, Version 2.7
codeSystems.description: FHIR Value set/code system definition for HL7 v2 table 0360 ver 2.9 ( Degree/License/Certificate)

codeSystems.name: ActCode
codeSystems.title: v3 Code System ActCode
codeSystems.description:
A code specifying the particular kind of Act that the Act-instance represents within its class.  Constraints:
The kind of Act (e.g. physical examination, serum potassium, inpatient encounter, charge financial transaction, etc.) is specified with a code from one of several, typically external, coding systems.  The coding system will depend on the class of Act, such as LOINC for observations, etc. Conceptually, the Act.code must be a specialization of the Act.classCode. This is why the structure of ActClass domain should be reflected in the superstructure of the ActCode domain and then individual codes or externally referenced vocabularies subordinated under these domains that reflect the ActClass structure. Act.classCode and Act.code are not modifiers of each other but the Act.code concept should really imply the Act.classCode concept. For a negative example, it is not appropriate to use an Act.code "potassium" together with and Act.classCode for "laboratory observation" to somehow mean "potassium laboratory observation" and then use the same Act.code for "potassium" together with Act.classCode for "medication" to mean "substitution of potassium". This mutually modifying use of Act.code and Act.classCode is not permitted.

codeSystems.name: ActConsentDirective
codeSystems.title: ActConsentDirective
codeSystems.description: ActConsentDirective codes are used to specify the type of Consent Directive to which a Consent Directive Act conforms.

codeSystems.name: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.title: ActEncounterCode
codeSystems.description: Domain provides codes that qualify the ActEncounterClass (ENC)

codeSystems.name: ActPharmacySupplyType
codeSystems.title: ActPharmacySupplyType
codeSystems.description: Identifies types of dispensing events

codeSystems.name: ActPriority
codeSystems.title: ActPriority
codeSystems.description:
A code or set of codes (e.g., for routine, emergency,) specifying the urgency under which the Act happened, can happen, is happening, is intended to happen, or is requested/demanded to happen.

*Discussion:* This attribute is used in orders to indicate the ordered priority, and in event documentation it indicates the actual priority used to perform the act. In definition mood it indicates the available priorities.

codeSystems.name: ActReason
codeSystems.title: v3 Code System ActReason
codeSystems.description:
A set of codes specifying the motivation, cause, or rationale of an Act, when such rationale is not reasonably represented as an ActRelationship of type "has reason" linking to another Act.  Examples:
Example reasons that might qualify for being coded in this field might be: "routine requirement", "infectious disease reporting requirement", "on patient request", "required by law".

codeSystems.name: ActSubstanceAdminSubstitutionCode
codeSystems.title: ActSubstanceAdminSubstitutionCode
codeSystems.description: ***No description***

codeSystems.name: HL7FormatCodes
codeSystems.title: HL7 ValueSet of Format Codes for use with Document Sharing
codeSystems.description: The HL7-FormatCodes value set is defined to be the set of FormatCode(s) defined by implementation guides published by HL7 and other SDOs. The use of a formatCode from the FormatCodes value set specifies the technical format that a document conforms to. The formatCode is a further specialization more detailed than the mime-type. The formatCode provides sufficient information to allow any potential document content consumer to know if it can process and/or display the content of the document based on the document encoding, structure and template conformance indicated by the formatCode. The set of formatCodes is intended to be extensible. The Content Logical Description is defined intentionally to permit formatCodes defined by other Standards Development Organizations to be added by inclusion of additional formatCode Code Systems.

codeSystems.name: ParticipationMode
codeSystems.title: ParticipationMode
codeSystems.description: Identifies the primary means by which an Entity participates in an Act.

codeSystems.name: PurposeOfUse
codeSystems.title: PurposeOfUse
codeSystems.description: Supports communication of purpose of use at a general level.

codeSystems.name: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.title: ServiceDeliveryLocationRoleType
codeSystems.description: A role of a place that further classifies the setting (e.g., accident site, road side, work site, community location) in which services are delivered.

codeSystems.name: SubstanceAdminSubstitutionReason
codeSystems.title: SubstanceAdminSubstitutionReason
codeSystems.description: ***No description***
